issues.title: Inconsistent absolute/relative imports for tid utility
issues.description: ReportTable.tsx imports 'tid' from 'shared' (bare import) while other files use '@/shared/infrastructure/config/tid' (absolute path). This inconsistency violates the rule that absolute imports should be preferred over relative for cross-layer imports.
issues.fix: Change 'import { tid } from "shared"' to 'import { tid } from "@/shared/infrastructure/config/tid"' for consistency with ReportsPage.tsx line 7 and ReportFiltersBar.tsx line 6

issues.title: Relative import of sibling component
issues.description: ReportTable.tsx uses relative import for OrderStatusBadge: 'import { OrderStatusBadge } from "./OrderStatusBadge"'. While this is within the presentation layer, the architecture rules prefer absolute imports for cross-layer clarity.
issues.fix: Change to absolute import: 'import { OrderStatusBadge } from "@/features/reports/presentation/components/OrderStatusBadge"'

issues.title: Mixed import path styles in infrastructure layer
issues.description: reportsApi.ts uses relative import '../domain/types' while other infrastructure files and the codebase generally uses absolute imports. This creates inconsistency.
issues.fix: Change 'import type { ReportFilters, ReportOrdersResponse } from "../domain/types"' to 'import type { ReportFilters, ReportOrdersResponse } from "@/features/reports/domain/types"'

issues.title: Missing barrel exports for layer organization
issues.description: The application/, domain/, infrastructure/, and presentation/ subdirectories lack index.ts barrel exports. Only the root feature exports ReportsPage. This violates the architecture rule that each layer should have index.ts barrel exports for cleaner imports and better encapsulation.
issues.fix: Create index.ts files in: application/, application/hooks/, application/utils/, domain/, infrastructure/, presentation/components/, presentation/pages/ to export public APIs from each layer

issues.title: Magic number for date ISO formatting
issues.description: Line 35 defines 'const ISO_DATE_LENGTH = 10' which is a magic number for ISO date slicing. While constants are defined locally, this could be moved to domain/constants.ts for consistency and reusability.
issues.fix: Add ISO_DATE_LENGTH to apps/admin/src/features/reports/domain/constants.ts and import it in ReportsPage.tsx

issues.title: Magic number for order ID preview length
issues.description: Line 10 defines 'const ORDER_ID_PREVIEW_LENGTH = 8' as a component-level constant. This presentation-specific constant could be in domain/constants.ts or a presentation-specific constants file.
issues.fix: Move ORDER_ID_PREVIEW_LENGTH to a presentation/constants.ts file or domain/constants.ts for centralized configuration management

issues.title: Tailwind style constants in presentation component
issues.description: OrderStatusBadge.tsx defines MUTED_STYLE and WARNING_STYLE as component-level constants. These styling constants could be centralized in a presentation constants or theme file.
issues.fix: Create apps/admin/src/features/reports/presentation/constants.ts to export style constants and import them in OrderStatusBadge.tsx

issues.title: XML/Excel hardcoded strings in utility
issues.description: Lines 22-98 contain hardcoded XML and Excel worksheet strings (WORKSHEET_CLOSE, XML headers, etc.). These could be extracted to domain/constants.ts as Excel export format constants.
issues.fix: Extract Excel XML constants to domain/constants.ts: EXCEL_WORKSHEET_CLOSE, EXCEL_HEADERS (array), EXCEL_XML_VERSION, EXCEL_WORKBOOK_XMLNS

exports: types.ts (OrderStatus, ReportOrderItem, ReportOrder, ReportFilters, UserOption, ProductOption)

application.note: Properly implements URL state management with nuqs

presentation.note: Minor import path inconsistency found (ReportTable.tsx uses bare 'shared' import)

nuqsUrlState.note: All filter parameters correctly use nuqs parseAs* functions

positiveFindings: Excellent layer separation with clear domain → application → presentation dependencies
URL state management properly uses nuqs for filter persistence
Appropriate use of absolute imports for cross-layer paths
Comprehensive E2E test coverage validating filter behavior and URL state
Clean component composition with proper prop drilling
ReportFiltersBar correctly handles filter state updates via URL
